T0152.title: Software Kill
T0152.definition: A scam where fraudsters directly control a victim's phone via remote access software to transfer funds.
T0152.description: Scammers, posing as customer service or law enforcement, trick victims into downloading a remote control app disguised as a legitimate tool and granting accessibility permissions. Once access is gained, the scammer can monitor the screen in real-time, intercept SMS messages, and directly operate the victim's mobile banking app to transfer money or make unauthorized purchases without the victim's knowledge.
T0152.usageExample: He downloaded a meeting app as instructed and shared his screen. Then the remote takeover happened; he watched helplessly as his phone moved on its own and his money was transferred out.
